payload.answer: Required accounting fields for evaluation: token counts (total tokens consumed), provider ID and model ID (to identify the source of tokens), estimated price (cost predicted from usage), and latency statistics (p50, p90, p95) describing response-time distribution. Relationships may include how token counts influence price and how latency metrics map to user-perceived performance. Do not assume exact pricing formula or provider specifics beyond stated IDs; if details are missing, retain as unknown/ambiguous.
payload.decision: provide a concise explanation of required accounting fields for evaluation: token counts, provider/model IDs, estimated price, and latency metrics (p50, p90, p95). preserve ambiguity where appropriate and avoid presenting superseded/deleted facts as current.

entity_resolution.reason: fields are foundational and may be updated; unresolved specifics (e.g., exact price calculation formula) should remain ambiguous until defined in contract/spec

memory_cards.statement: Evaluation accounting fields are needed to quantify cost, provenance, and performance of resource usage: token counts, provider/model identifiers, estimated price, and latency distribution (p50, p90, p95).

details: Ambiguities preserved where definitions are not yet provided

repair_options: Add precise schema definitions for token counting (per-call vs cumulative).
Specify how estimated_price is calculated (pricing model, currency, unit).
Define how latency metrics are measured (end-to-end vs backend, warm/cold starts).
List allowed provider_id/model_id formats and validation rules.
